DEFENDANT NAVISTAR, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ARE DENIED. DR. BERMAN'S TESTIMONY CONFIRMS THAT HE DID NOT DIAGNOSE PLAINTIFF AS HAVING AN ASBESTOS DISEASE BUT SENT HIM TO A SPECIALIST. (DEPO 29-30). CONSTRUING THE EVIDENCE IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF, HE DID NOT HAVE REASON TO KNOW OF THE ASBESTOS CAUSED DISEASE UNTIL DR. MASTERS' DIAGNOSIS. NAVISTAR'S SOPHISTICATED USER ARGUMENT IS WITHOUT MERIT. PARTIES ARE TO MEET AND CONFER IN PREPARING A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK MAHONEY; REPORTER: KIMBERLY JENNINGS, CSR 11452

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ALL CARS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED FROM JUN-20-2008 TO JUN-25-2008 ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. JUDGE: PAUL H. ALVARADO, REPORTER: JACQUELINE CHAN, CSR 10276

DEFENDANT MAREMONT CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S DISCOVERY RESPONSES ON PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY IS NOT FACTUALLY DEVOID. IF IT WERE, TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT ARE CREATED BY THE DECLARATIONS OF ROACH, COHEN AND KOSICH. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO MARKET SHARE THEORY, MISREPRESENTATION CLAIM AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF AND PLAINTIFF DID NOT CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK MAHONEY; REPORTER: KIMBERLY JENNINGS, CSR 11452

DEFENDANT SAN PABLO AUTOMOTIVE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ARE DENIED. DR. BERMAN'S TESTIMONY CONFIRMS THAT HE DID NOT DIAGNOSE PLAINTIFF AS HAVING AN ASBESTOS DISEASE BUT SENT HIM TO A SPECIALIST. (DEPO 29-30). CONSTRUING THE EVIDENCE IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF, HE DID NOT HAVE REASON TO KNOW OF THE ASBESTOS CAUSED DISEASE UNTIL DR. MASTERS' DIAGNOSIS. NAVISTAR'S SOPHISTICATED USER ARGUMENT IS WITHOUT MERIT. PARTIES ARE TO MEET AND CONFER IN PREPARING A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK MAHONEY; REPORTER: KIMBERLY JENNINGS, CSR 11452

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Mar-14-2012 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Judge Suzanne Bolanos, Clerk Karissa Shaw, Not Reported. (602)

***This is the lead case and has been consolidate for all purposes with case 275663.***

APPEARANCES:
Jennifer Alesio of Brayton Purcell representing the plaintiff.
Brien McMahon and Richard Chon of Perkins Coie representing Honeywell International, Inc.
Kevin Mayer of Crower & Moring representing Honeywell International, Inc.
Camille Fong of Lewis Brisbois representing STM Automotive.

At 2:00 p.m., this matter comes on calendar today for a pre-trial conference. The remaining defendants in this matter are Honeywell Internation, Inc. and STM Automotive. Court and counsel confer on the trial schedule as well as jury selection.

Hardshipping will be conducted on 3/15/12 at 9:30 a.m. and possibly 3/16/12. "Mini Openings" (4 minutes per side) will be presented by counsel. Voir Dire will be conducted on 3/29/12 at 9:30 a.m. and possibly on 4/2/12. Each side is allowed one (1) hour of voir dire. Evidence will start either on 4/2/12 or 4/3/12, depending on when a jury is sworn to try the cause. Court and counsel will further confer on the length of opening statements on 3/23/12.

There are approximately five (5) motions in limine to be heard before opening statements (including a motion to bifurcate). Oppositions to these motions are due by 3/19/12. Argument will be heard on 3/23/12 at 9:30 a.m. Also, page and line designations as well as counter-designations are due by 3/19/12.

At 2:38 p.m., this matter is adjourned until March 15, 2012 at 9:30 a.m. for jury hardshipping.

SUMMARY OF DATES:
* 3/15/12 & 3/16/12 - Jury Hardshipping

* 3/19/12 - Page/Line designations & counter-designations + oppositions to MILs (to be heard before opening statements) are due

* 3/23/12 - Hearing on MILs that must be ruled upon before opening statements + conference on length of opening statements

* 3/29/12 & 4/2/12 - Voir Dire

* 4/2/12 or 4/3/12 - Evidence begins

ASBESTOS JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Mar-08-2012 Continued To Mar-15-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 602
MINI MINUTES: Judge Suzanne Bolanos, Clerk Karissa Shaw, Reporter Patricia Rodriguez CSR #12436. (602)

(This matter is the lead case and has been consolidated for all purposes with 275663.)

APPEARANCES:
Jennifer Alesio & James Nevin of Brayton Purcell representing the plaintiff.
Rhonda Woo of Selman Breitman representing California Equipment Co.
Camille Fong representing STM Automotive.
Richard Chou & Brien McMahon of Perkins Coie representing Honeywell International Inc.
Barry Endrick of Hassard Bonnington LLP representing Honeywell Internation Inc.

This matter has been singly-assigned for jury trial by Master Calendar. The court conducts a pre-trial conference this day.

At 1:30 p.m., court and counsel confer on the statute of limitations issue. Counsel must report to Department 502 on 3/9/12 at 9:30 a.m. to reach an agreement regarding a possible extension on the statute requirement. Jury selection is postposed until 3/15/12 (unless a settlement of the entire case is reached beforehand).

At 1:48 p.m., this matter is adjourned.

ASBESTOS JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Mar-07-2012 Continued To Mar-08-12 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 602
MINI MINUTES: Judge Suzanne Bolanos, Clerk Karissa Shaw, Not Reported. (602)

(This matter is the lead case and has been consolidated for all purposes with 275663.)

APPEARANCES:
Jennifer Alesio of Brayton Purcell representing the plaintiff.
James Ostertag representing Mack Trucks, Inc.
Camille Fong representing STM Automotive.
Richard Chou representing Honeywell.

This matter has been singly-assigned for jury trial by Master Calendar. The court conducts a pre-trial conference this day.

At 10:05 a.m., court and counsel confer on the trial schedule; jury hardshipping to be conducted 3/9/12 (at 9:30 a.m.), voir dire will be conducted 3/12/12 (at 9:30 a.m.), motions in limine to be filed today and oppositions to be filed by 3/9/12.

The settlement deadline is 3/8/12 at 2:00 p.m.

Counsel is to be present at the first part of jury hardshipping when the court is addressing the jurors regarding the statement of the case and the hardship process. The joint statement of the case is due by close of business 3/8/12 and 150 copies of the juror questionnaire are due by Friday morning (3/9/12).

Counsel estimate the trial to last a maximum of 15 court days. There are only three remaining defendants in both cases; Mack Trucks, Inc., STM Automotive and Honeywell. Court and counsel confer on the statute of limitations issue as to whether the last day is 3/9/12 or 3/12/12.

Page and line designations are due by 3/9/12 and counter-designations/objections due by 3/14/12.

At 10:26 a.m., this matter is adjourned until Thursday, March 8, 2012, at 1:30 p.m. for a further pre-trial conference.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-08-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PATTERSON PARTS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances:
Laurel Halbany of Brayton Purcell (898-1555) for plaintiff and responding party
Ernest Faitos of Selman Breitman (979-0400) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: motion for summary judgment and summary adjudication are denied. Dr. Berman's testimony confirms that he did not diagnose plaintiff as having an asbestos disease but sent him to a specialist. (Depo 29-30). Construing the evidence in favor of plaintiff, he did not have reason to know of the asbestos caused disease until Dr. Masters' diagnosis. Navistar's sophisticated user argument is without merit. Parties are to meet and confer in preparing a form of order. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Kimberly Jennings, CSR 11452

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-07-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MAREMONT CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Appearances:
Ron Archer of Brayton Purcell (898-1555) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff and responding party
Michelle Jackson of McKenna Long & Aldridge (267-4132) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Plaintiff's discovery responses on plaintiff's deposition testimony is not factually devoid. If it were, triable issues of fact are created by the declarations of Roach, Cohen and Kosich. Motion for summary adjudication is granted as to market share theory, misrepresentation claim and punitive damages. Defendant shifted the burden of proof and plaintiff did not create a triable issue of fact. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/PB)Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Kimberly Jennings, CSR 11452
